title: Mashups
body: Well, as I already mentioned in [this post](/taller-comunidades-sociales/ "Social communities workshop") from last week, this Saturday we attended a workshop at the [i-dissabtes](http://www.pimpampum.net/i-dissabtes/ "i-dissabtes") at CaixaForum. In a very engaging presentation full of examples [PimPamPum](http://www.pimpampum.net "PimPamPum. Virtual communities. Interactive projects. New media advertising. Interaction design. Mobile applications. Creative coding.") showed us a wide variety of applications made with the APIs offered by Amazon, Google, YouTube, eBay, FaceBook or Flickr. Most of the time [these applications](http://www.pimpampum.net/i-dissabtes/mashups/links.html "Links to xarop"), generally created by people eager to create new things and not seeking any immediate benefit, are very visual, attractive, usable and even fun. Many times they are nothing more than games, but all of them have functionalities that can be very useful to us since the original applications do not have them or even make it much easier to perform the most common tasks. They made special mention of the *xarop* created with the Flickr API, in which they are specialists with their [FlickrToys](http://www.pimpampum.net/toys/ "Various applications with Flickr photos"). They ended the session by explaining [how we can make our own *xarop*](http://www.pimpampum.net/i-dissabtes/mashups/howto.html). For more information you can visit the page they created for this workshop: [Introduction to xarop with Flickr](http://www.pimpampum.net/i-dissabtes/mashups/index.php "Introduction to xarop with Flickr")
